summary:
The claim is that B-cell lymphomas are the most common type of Non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma (NHL) in the orbit. This is supported by evidence showing B-cell NHLs constitute >85% of all NHL cases globally and have a higher likelihood of extranodal presentation, particularly in regions adjacent to the orbit (e.g., paranasal sinuses). These factors make B-cell variants more frequently observed in orbital locations compared to other NHL subtypes. Based on this alignment of broader epidemiological trends with localized pathophysiology, the answer choice is A.
Answer Choice: $\boxed{\text{A}}$
